title: The Workflow: Automating Meeting Notes to Action Items
subject: From meeting chaos to organized action items in 3 minutes flat
preheader: The Fireflies + Notion + Zapier workflow that changed how we run meetings
bodyHtml:
The Workflow

AI Productivity Hub · April 2026

From Meeting Chaos to Organized Action Items in 3 Minutes

The average knowledge worker spends 31 hours per month in unproductive meetings. The biggest culprit isn’t the meetings themselves — it’s what happens after. Action items get lost, notes get scattered, and the same decisions get relitigated a week later. Here’s the three-tool workflow that fixes it.

What You’ll Need

1. Fireflies.ai — AI meeting recorder ($10/mo)
2. Notion — Workspace for task management (free tier works)
3. Zapier — Automation glue ($19.99/mo)

Total cost: ~$30/month. Time saved: 5-8 hours/week.

Step 1

Set Up Fireflies.ai as Your Meeting Recorder

Connect Fireflies to your Google Calendar or Outlook. It will auto-join every meeting as a silent participant, recording and transcribing in real time. The key setting: enable “Auto-generate action items” and “Meeting summary” in your Fireflies dashboard under AI Apps. This is what turns a raw transcript into structured data. Fireflies.ai setup guide →

Step 2

Create Your Notion Action Items Database

Create a Notion database with these columns: Task (title), Owner (person), Due Date (date), Meeting Source (text), Status (select: To Do, In Progress, Done), and Priority (select: High, Medium, Low). This becomes your single source of truth for every commitment made in every meeting. Notion AI tips →

Step 3

Connect Fireflies to Notion via Zapier

Create a Zap with this trigger/action chain:

Trigger: Fireflies → New Transcription Completed
Action 1: Formatter → Split the action items field into individual items
Action 2: Looping → For each action item, create a new row in your Notion database
Action 3: Notion → Create page with Task, Meeting Source (meeting title), and Status “To Do”

The result: within 3 minutes of your meeting ending, every action item appears in Notion, tagged with the meeting it came from, ready to be assigned and prioritized. Zapier workflow templates →

Pro Tip

Add a Weekly Review Automation

Set up a second Zap: every Friday at 9 AM, pull all “To Do” items from Notion and send a digest to yourself (or your team via Slack). This prevents the classic trap where action items get created but never reviewed. You can also use Notion AI to summarize the week’s completed items into a status update you can share with stakeholders.

Related Reading

Fireflies vs Otter.ai: Which Meeting Assistant?

Not sure which meeting recorder to use? We tested both on accuracy, features, and integrations. See which one fits your workflow better.

Read the Comparison →

This workflow takes about 30 minutes to set up and pays for itself after your first meeting. Try it this week and reply with how it goes — we feature the best reader setups in future issues.
